c1-hedging.title: Academic Hedging Language
c1-hedging.pattern: [claim] + деп болжауға болады | [claim] + деуге негіз бар
c1-hedging.explanation: Formal and academic Kazakh softens strong claims with hedging expressions that signal caution rather than certainty. Деп болжауға болады ("it can be hypothesized that") frames a claim as a reasoned guess: Бұл құбылыс климатқа байланысты деп болжауға болады. Деуге негіз бар ("there is grounds to say that") signals that evidence supports a claim without asserting it outright.
c1-hedging.fact: In scholarly Kazakh writing, as in English, overusing unhedged, absolute claims reads as naive or unscientific, while careful hedges like болжауға болады are what mark a text as rigorous, evidence-based argumentation rather than casual opinion.

dialogue.native: Толық сенімді деп айту қиын, бірақ негіз бар.
dialogue.en: It's hard to say fully reliable, but there is some ground for it.

c1-historical-narrative.title: Historical Narrative Register
c1-historical-narrative.pattern: [-ды/-ті simple past] alternating with [-атын/-етін habitual] and [-ып жатты progressive past]
c1-historical-narrative.explanation: Formal historical writing weaves together multiple past tenses to build a layered narrative: the simple past (-ды/-ті) marks discrete completed events (1991 жылы Қазақстан тәуелсіздік алды), the habitual past (-атын/-етін) frames ongoing background conditions of the era, and the past progressive (-ып жатты) depicts action unfolding at a narrated moment.
c1-historical-narrative.fact: This tense-layering is what separates a bare timeline from genuine historical narrative — a text using only -ды/-ті reads like a list of dates, while alternating in -атын/-етін for "what things were generally like" gives the reader a sense of lived, ongoing texture behind the punctual events.

dialogue.en: What were the first years of independence like?

dialogue.native: Қиын кез еді, бірақ халық жаңа өмірге бейімделіп жатты.
dialogue.en: It was a difficult time, but the people were adapting to a new life.

c1-literary-parallelism.title: Literary Parallelism and Rhetorical Devices
c1-literary-parallelism.pattern: [clause A] ..., [structurally mirrored clause B] ... (often with antithesis)
c1-literary-parallelism.explanation: Literary and rhetorical Kazakh — speeches, poetry, formal essays — relies on syntactic parallelism, where clauses share the same grammatical shape to create rhythm and reinforce a contrast: Өткенді өзгерте алмаймыз, болашаққа қараймыз (We cannot change the past, we look to the future) mirrors "cannot X" against "do Y." Antithesis (pairing opposites) is a favorite device for building memorable lines.
c1-literary-parallelism.fact: This balanced, mirrored phrasing draws especially on the oral tradition of the zhyrau and akyn poet-singers, whose improvised verse relied on parallel structure to be memorable and easy to hold in memory without writing it down.

dialogue.native: Сіздің өміріңіздегі ең маңызды қағида қандай?
dialogue.en: What is the most important principle in your life?
We cannot change the past, we look to the future.

items.kk: Бұл құбылыс климатқа байланысты деп болжауға болады.
items.en: It can be hypothesized that this phenomenon is related to climate.
There is reason to say the results are correct.

items.kk: Бұл теория дәлелденген деп айту қиын.
items.en: It is difficult to say that this theory has been proven.

items.kk: Зерттеу нәтижелері болжамды растайды деуге болады.
items.en: It can be said that the research results confirm the hypothesis.

items.kk: Бұл фактор шешуші рөл атқарған болуы мүмкін.
items.en: This factor may have played a decisive role.
It can be considered that the data are insufficient.

items.kk: Бұл құбылысты толығымен түсіндірді деу қате болар еді.
items.en: It would be a mistake to say this fully explains the phenomenon.
On this basis, a conclusion can be drawn.
This result may be a coincidence.
There is no ground to say the evidence is sufficient.
In 1991, Kazakhstan gained independence.

items.kk: Ол кездері ел экономикалық қиындық көретін.
items.en: In those days, the country used to face economic difficulties.
Reforms gradually began to be implemented.
The people were adapting to a new life.
In 1997, the capital was moved from Almaty to Astana.
At that time, many enterprises used to close down.
The country began adopting new laws.
The economy gradually began to stabilize.

items.kk: Сол жылдары адамдар жұмыс іздеп жүретін.
items.en: In those years, people used to search for work.

items.kk: 2015 жылы Қазақстан Дүниежүзілік сауда ұйымына мүше болды.
items.en: In 2015, Kazakhstan became a member of the World Trade Organization.
We cannot change the past, we look to the future.
Knowledge is light, ignorance is darkness.

items.kk: Тіл — ұлттың жаны, жер — ұлттың тәні.
items.en: Language is the nation's soul, land is the nation's body.

items.kk: Кешегі күн — тәжірибе, бүгінгі күн — мүмкіндік.
items.en: Yesterday is experience, today is opportunity.

items.kk: Батыр — елі үшін, ақын — тілі үшін жасайды.
items.en: A hero lives for his country, a poet lives for his language.
In unity there is strength, in discord there is sorrow.

items.kk: Ерте тұрған — ырысқа жетер, кеш тұрған — өкінішке қалар.
items.en: He who rises early attains fortune, he who rises late is left with regret.

items.kk: Досыңды сынау — қиын емес, досыңды сақтау — қиын.
items.en: Testing your friend is not hard, keeping your friend is hard.
Youth is fire, old age is wise counsel.
Speech is silver, silence is golden.
